duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Missing claimId == edgeId validation in checkClaimIdLink allows cross-rival timer cache inflation via updateTimerCacheByClaim
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All five describe the same code defect: checkClaimIdLink only verifies mutualId == originId and level adjacency but never verifies claimId == edgeId, allowing dishonest edges to inherit timer from honest claiming edges. ECON-H001 selected for its natspec discrepancy evidence, detailed economics, and comprehensive attack scenario.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Rollup solvency violation: stakeOnNewAssertion transfers requiredStake to loserStakeEscrow without reducing staker amountStaked, creating phantom withdrawable funds
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All 14 entries describe the same root cause: non-first-child assertion creation sends requiredStake to loserStakeEscrow without reducing amountStaked, leading to phantom withdrawable funds, accounting desync, and potential insolvency. ECON-H003 (permanent inflation), ECON2-H008 (compound drain via multiple forks), and ECON2-H013 (phantom funds) are all consequences of this same defect. NOTE: TECH2-H003 has two entries with different content; only the 'staking insolvency' variant is removed here; the 'machineStep' variant is a distinct unique threat.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Excess stake above requiredStake permanently locked for losing validators with no recovery path
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All four describe the same issue: validators who deposit more than requiredStake and lose have their excess permanently locked because their assertion never gets a child (requireInactiveStaker fails). ECON2-H002 selected for HIGH severity and economic analysis.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: VALIDATOR_AFK_BLOCKS hardcoded for 12-second block time causes premature whitelist removal on faster chains
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All five describe the same defect: VALIDATOR_AFK_BLOCKS = 201600 assumes 12s blocks; on faster chains the 28-day AFK window shrinks dramatically, enabling premature permissionless whitelist removal. OPS-H011 selected for HIGH severity and comprehensive analysis.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: removeWhitelistAfterValidatorAfk callable during contract pause (missing whenNotPaused modifier) enables permanent whitelist removal during legitimate pauses
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All six describe the same defect: removeWhitelistAfterValidatorAfk lacks whenNotPaused modifier; block.number continues during pause; after VALIDATOR_AFK_BLOCKS the whitelist is permanently disabled. NOTE: TECH2-H004 has two entries; only the 'pause/AFK' variant is in this group.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: First edge creator in createLayerZeroEdge gets retained stake advantage; honest validator who creates second loses stake to excessStakeReceiver
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both describe the same economic disadvantage: first edge's stake stays in contract (for refund), second goes to excessStakeReceiver. Honest validator who is second loses their original stake with no guaranteed recovery. TECH-H008 selected for higher severity and more detailed mechanism analysis.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Fee-on-transfer stake token causes EdgeChallengeManager refund insolvency
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All three describe the same issue in EdgeChallengeManager: safeTransferFrom receives less than stakeAmounts[level] with FoT tokens but refundStake attempts full amount, causing gradual balance drain and eventual refund failures. ECON-H013 selected for HIGH severity and clear investigation rationale.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Fee-on-transfer / non-standard stake token breaks RollupUserLogic staking solvency and accounting
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All three describe non-standard stakeToken causing accounting mismatch in RollupUserLogic: deposits credit nominal amounts while contract receives less, creating systemic insolvency. NOTE: TECH2-H004 'FoT/CEI' variant is removed here; the CEI concern is separately covered by OPS2-H002. OPS2-H010 spans both contracts but overlaps primarily here.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Zero stakeAmounts configuration allows costless layer zero edge spam at affected levels
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both describe the same issue: stakeAmounts[level] == 0 skips stake transfer, enabling costless edge creation and DoS on honest validators. ECON-H014 selected for HIGH severity and clearer investigation rationale.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: getPrevAssertionHash traversal reverts if intermediate firstRivals returns UNRIVALED sentinel value
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All three describe the same issue: getPrevAssertionHash uses firstRivals as edge ID without checking for UNRIVALED sentinel, causing revert that blocks confirmEdgeByOneStepProof. ECON-H010 selected for most specific description and economics.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: EdgeChallengeManager lacks pause mechanism; timers accumulate during rollup pause enabling instant malicious confirmation on unpause
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both describe the same architectural gap: EdgeChallengeManager has no Pausable and continues operating during RollupUserLogic pause. ECON2-H003 selected for HIGH severity and specific exploit scenario (timer accumulation enabling instant confirmation).

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Assertion confirmation can use stale/upgraded challenge manager via configHash binding to proxy address rather than implementation
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both target the same code point: confirmAssertion uses prevConfig.challengeManager validated against configHash. ECON-H006 covers redeployment scenarios while OPS-H010 covers proxy upgrade scenarios; same root cause (configHash validates address not behavior). ECON-H006 selected for higher severity and detailed economics.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: machineStep overflow in confirmEdgeByOneStepProof with high numBigStepLevel configuration
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both describe the same issue: stepSize *= bigStepHeight loop overflows uint256 for high numBigStepLevel, blocking one-step proof confirmation. OPS-H007 selected for more detailed parameter threshold analysis.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Gas griefing via expensive Merkle prefix proof verification in bisectEdge/createLayerZeroEdge
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All four describe the same DoS vector: MerkleTreeLib.verifyPrefixProof loops and hashing operations can be forced to high gas cost via crafted proof arrays, griefing honest validators during active disputes. TECH-H014 selected for most comprehensive analysis including config-dependency and economic context.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Permissionless whitelist removal via removeWhitelistAfterFork on L2 chain ID changes
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both describe the same issue: removeWhitelistAfterFork permanently disables whitelist if block.chainid changes, which can happen on L2 upgrades/migrations. ECON2-H009 selected for higher severity and detailed economics.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Unbounded loop in multiUpdateTimeCacheByChildren enables gas-based DoS
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both describe the same issue: multiUpdateTimeCacheByChildren iterates user-supplied edgeIds array with no length cap, enabling gas griefing. TECH2-H005 selected for slightly more specific analysis.
